_note: Refreshed by refresh-outdoor-feed. Ski focus Dec-Apr, backpacking May-Oct, mixed in shoulder weeks. Western US bias with world highlights.

items.headline: Mammoth Mountain, CA, to Stay Open Through Memorial Day Again After 7+ Feet of February Snow
items.snark: Mammoth's second season is the best North American ski deal nobody books, and Main Lodge is spinning lifts through Memorial Day weekend on a snowpack February rebuilt from scratch.

items.headline: Western US Ski Forecast: April 14-17, 2026
items.snark: Cascades and Canadian Rockies took the first round, then Idaho and Montana, and the Wasatch cleaned up on Friday. Grand Targhee inherited the tail on cold fluffy snow. This late in April that is a gift.

items.headline: I Skied Palisades Tahoe's Miracle April Storm, but Mother Nature Made It Challenging
items.snark: Palisades pulled 43 inches out of one April storm. That is not a spring tune up, that is a full midwinter reset on KT-22 and the Headwall.

items.headline: Heavenly Resort Reopening: Lake Tahoe Late-Season Skiing
items.snark: Heavenly fired the lifts back up April 18 and 19 after the Sierra hammer. Nobody on the pass calendar saw that weekend coming.

items.headline: The 9 Best Ski Resorts Still Open In North America
items.snark: A-Basin, Mammoth, Snowbird, Palisades, Targhee, and four more still spinning lifts. If there is a weekend left in this season, one of these nine is where it should go.

items.headline: New Film Captures The Allure And Perils Of Skiing Tuckerman Ravine
items.snark: The Proving Ground is the Tuck's film people will quote all summer. The Lip on a bluebird day is still the East Coast rite of passage.

items.headline: Arapahoe Basin Ends Parking Reservations As Spring Comes Early
items.snark: The Beach at A-Basin is wide open again, tailgates and slush laps on the East Wall, and this is the year closing day drifts deep into June.

items.headline: Ski Portillo, Chile, Opens June 20, Here is Everything New for 2026
items.snark: Portillo opens June 20 and every North American pro is already booked. A bunk at the Hotel with the Laguna del Inca out the window is a career ski season, not a vacation.

items.headline: The Weminuche High Route
items.snark: Durango to Silverton the hard way, linking Chicago Basin fourteeners and the Needle Range. With this year's thin San Juan snowpack the whole line could go by mid July.

items.headline: Will 2026 Be a High Snow Year on the PCT? What We Know So Far
items.snark: Klamath Basin sitting at 4 percent of median, Sierra entry drifting to late June. This year's PCT story is water scarcity and smoke, not postholes.

items.headline: Introductions, and Why I am Hiking the CDT in 2026
items.snark: A CDT hopeful bumped a May 1 start to April 15 because the San Juans barely have a snowpack. The Gila is already dry, cottonwoods leafing out, and ready.

items.headline: Rip and Go: Horseshoe Lake, Pecos Wilderness, NM
items.snark: Horseshoe Lake is the 3 day loop that sells Taos to the rest of the country, 5 miles of climb to alpine tarns under the Truchas Peaks. Thaw is early this year and the trail will be ready soon.
